summary: Large decomp-matching PR for common fighter ftCo_0A01. It replaced many placeholder/generated sections in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c with C implementations, added stage/camera/vector includes, introduced narrow noinline/dontinline wrapper helpers, rewrote some gotos into loops/switches, and updated ftCo_0A01.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures. The bot report records +7324 matched-code bytes, 11 new matches, 25 unmatched-item improvements, and one ftCo_800A2718 regression.

key_files.role: Main decomp source file; large body additions for ftCo_0A01 functions, new includes, structured control flow, stage-specific checks, collision queries, and matching wrapper helpers.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftCo_0A01.c +4922/-841, 20 hunks
diff.diff: added cm/camera.h, grcastle.h, grgreatbay.h, grrcruise.h, lbvector.h includes
diff.diff: ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800A3908 bodies replace placeholder comments/generated-looking code
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: bot report lists 11 new matches and 25 improvements in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup; several static prototypes were changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete return and argument types.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftCo_0A01.h +10/-10, 3 hunks
diff.diff header hunk: ftCo_800A229C bool->s32, ftCo_800AEA8C UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS->void(Fighter*), ftCo_800A2C80 UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS->s32(Fighter*)
diff.diff header hunk: ftCo_800A6A98, ftCo_800A6D2C, ftCo_800A6FC4, ftCo_800A8210, ftCo_800A866C signatures updated

what_changed: Added concrete C bodies for previously placeholder-marked functions including ftCo_800A229C and ftCo_800A2C80 in the loaded diff excerpt.
Added large partial implementations for functions such as ftCo_800A3908 and later ftCo_0A01 functions, producing broad matched-code progress according to the bot report.
Introduced include dependencies for camera, Great Bay, Castle, Rainbow Cruise, and lbvector helpers used by the new source.
Reworked a generated-looking item traversal with labels/gotos into a for loop over HSD_GObj_Entities->items with continue/return structure.
Reworked stage dispatch around STORY, ZEBES, ONETT, RCRUISE, BIGBLUE, and ICEMTN into switch/case or direct conditional forms.
Added wrapper helpers named with _noinline and _dontinline around existing functions such as ftCo_800A1B38, ftCo_800A1C44, ftCo_800A1F3C, ftCo_800A2718, and ftCo_IsGrabbing.
Updated ftCo_0A01.h prototypes to concrete return types and argument lists for several static functions.
Bot report records matched code rising to 68.42%, +0.19%, +7324 bytes, with 11 new matched functions and 25 improved unmatched items.
Bot report also records one unmatched-item regression: ftCo_800A2718 decreased from 82.34% to 77.32%.
smart_moves: Used real subsystem includes rather than source-local declarations for newly referenced helpers, consistent with truthful include/header practice.
Converted the item list traversal from label/goto form into a natural for loop while preserving the explicit item filtering sequence visible in the diff.
Used switch-style stage dispatch for stage_info.internal_stage_id in helper logic, making stage-specific behavior more searchable and reviewable.
Replaced multiple unknown header prototypes with concrete signatures when the new function bodies exposed return and argument types.
Kept stage-specific helper names visible at callsites, such as grGreatBay_801F66A4, grCastle_801CDF54, grLib_801C9E60, and Stage_GetBlastZone* accessors, instead of hiding them behind raw offsets.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves a static prototype, update the owning header from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the concrete signature in the same PR.
evidence_refs: diff.diff header hunk: ftCo_800A2C80 changed to static s32 ftCo_800A2C80(Fighter* fp)
diff.diff header hunk: ftCo_800A6A98/ftCo_800A6D2C changed to s32(Fighter*, Vec3*)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated-looking label/goto traversal can often be rewritten as natural C loops and switch dispatch when the repeated structure is clear.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: inlineL0 changed from loop_8/block_3/block_7 labels to for (cur = HSD_GObj_Entities->items; cur != NULL; cur = cur->next)
diff.diff: inlineL0 stage_info.internal_stage_id handling rewritten as switch over STORY/ZEBES/ONETT/default

decomp_lessons.lesson: For common fighter recovery/collision logic, keeping stage and collision helper calls explicit can make recovered behavior reviewable while still matching many functions.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A229C uses grGreatBay_801F66A4, grCastle_801CDF54, Stage_GetBlastZone* and Camera_8003118C
diff.diff: ftCo_800A2C80 uses mpCheckFloor, mpCheckLeftWall, mpCheckRightWall, lbVector_Normalize, and stage line filters
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: bot report shows several ftCo_0A01 functions newly matched or improved

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for unknown common fighter functions, e.g. ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800A3908, rather than inventing semantics.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: implemented functions keep ftCo_80xxxx address-style names
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary wrapper helpers use suffixes such as _noinline, _dontinline, _dontinline2, and _dontinline3 to signal matching-oriented call-shape control rather than semantic meaning.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A1B38_noinline, ftCo_800A1C44_dontinline, ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline2, ftCo_800A2718_dontinline3

naming_conventions.convention: Local variable names in the loaded excerpt are mostly role/shape names such as fp, arg1, cur, cur_ip, line_id, floor_pos, floor_normal, stage, margin, bottom, and data.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A229C, inlineL0, ftCo_800A2C80 variable declarations

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow noinline/dontinline wrapper functions were added around existing helpers to influence call/inlining shape.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A1B38_noinline returns ftCo_800A1B38(arg0)
diff.diff: ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline and ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline2 forward to ftCo_800A1F3C
diff.diff: ftCo_800A2718_dontinline chain
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence shows the wrappers and overall bot progress, but does not include objdiff rationale for each wrapper. Treat as an observed matching tactic, not a general accepted style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural loop conversion for an item linked-list scan replaced label/goto blocks while retaining item checks and early returns.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: inlineL0 loop over HSD_GObj_Entities->items
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The bot report is aggregate; it does not isolate this loop as the cause of a specific function's match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Stage-specific switch dispatch and explicit gotos inside complex cases were used in ftCo_800A229C to express control flow while preserving target structure.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A229C switch(stage) with RCRUISE, BIGBLUE, ICEMTN and block_43
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Some labels remain inside the recovered C, so this is partial structuring rather than full cleanup.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Blast-zone boundary checks are repeated explicitly around floor and wall collision queries in ftCo_800A2C80.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A2C80 repeats oob checks after mpCheckFloor, mpCheckLeftWall, and mpCheckRightWall
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No evidence in the slice shows whether these repeated blocks were tested as a helper or macro; do not generalize beyond this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftCo_0A01 decomp PR combined body recovery with header prototype cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2524 recovered substantial common fighter ftCo_0A01 code and updated ftCo_0A01.h prototypes from unknown forms to concrete signatures where function bodies exposed the types. Bot evidence reports +7324 matched-code bytes, 11 new matches, and 25 improvements, with one ftCo_800A2718 regression.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl
diff.diff header hunks
text_corpus.jsonl bot report

accepted_candidate_records.title: Observed ftCo_0A01 matching tactic: narrow noinline/dontinline forwarding wrappers
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff adds forwarding wrappers with _noinline/_dontinline suffixes around existing ftCo helpers. This is evidence that the PR used wrapper call-shape control in this file, but the slice does not provide isolated objdiff proof for each wrapper.
evidence_refs: diff.diff wrapper additions
text_corpus.jsonl aggregate bot report

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit evidence for persistent noinline/dontinline forwarding wrappers
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2524 shows multiple narrow forwarding wrappers named _noinline/_dontinline in matching source. Curator may consider whether the standards should explicitly list these wrapper forms alongside PAD_STACK, declaration-order changes, dummy locals, and other tactics that need narrow objdiff evidence before remaining in reviewable source.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800A1B38_noinline, ftCo_800A1F3C_noinline, ftCo_800A2718_dontinline helpers
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards cover matching tactics broadly but do not explicitly name noinline/dontinline forwarding wrappers; this PR provides an example for curator review, not automatic promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer from merge status that all _noinline/_dontinline wrappers are globally preferred style; the slice lacks isolated reviewer or objdiff rationale.
Do not claim ftCo_800A2718 was improved; bot evidence reports it as a regression in an unmatched item.
Do not invent semantic names for address-style ftCo functions; the PR keeps address-style names and gives no semantic naming rationale.
Do not claim the entire ftCo_0A01 translation unit matched; the bot report lists several unmatched-item improvements rather than full TU completion.
